items.title: OAuth support for the AWS MCP Server
items.content_snippet: You can now connect AI agents directly to the AWS MCP Server using AWS Sign-In. Agents connect using industry-standard OAuth without requiring additional authentication software. Existing AWS identities, sign-in methods, IAM permissions, and governance controls you have already set up continue to apply. Developers can authorize agents interactively through a browser or programmatically using non-interactive (headless) authorization. Administrators can govern OAuth access using familiar IAM polic

items.title: Amazon Timestream for InfluxDB now publishes database state change events to Amazon EventBridge
items.content_snippet: Amazon Timestream for InfluxDB now publishes events to Amazon EventBridge when your database instances or clusters undergo state changes. Events are emitted for lifecycle operations including creation, deletion, compute and storage scaling, parameter group updates, maintenance windows, and reboot — covering both successful completions and failures. With this capability, customers can use Amazon EventBridge rules to programmatically react to database operations without polling the API for status.

items.title: AWS Client VPN extends availability to four additional AWS Regions
items.content_snippet: AWS Client VPN is now available in four new regions: Canada West (Calgary), Mexico (Central) and two in Asia Pacific - New Zealand and Taipei. This fully managed service enables customers to securely connect their remote workforce to resources in AWS or on-premises networks. AWS Client VPN eliminates the need for hardware VPN appliances and complex operational management through its pay-as-you-go model. Organizations can easily manage and monitor VPN connections through a single console. To lear

items.title: Amazon SageMaker Unified Studio adds custom asset types to the catalog in IAM-based domains
items.content_snippet: Amazon SageMaker Unified Studio now supports custom asset types for IAM-based domains. With custom asset types, domain administrators can catalog any format of asset within the SageMaker Unified Studio, such as medical imaging files in Amazon S3, revenue dashboards built in PowerBI, or PDF research reports generated by a third-party platform. Custom asset types bring all assets, regardless of their underlying format, into the SageMaker catalog so teams can search, discover, and subscribe to them

items.title: Amazon SageMaker HyperPod now supports deep health checks for Slurm clusters with continuous provisioning
items.content_snippet: Amazon SageMaker HyperPod now supports deep health checks for Slurm-orchestrated clusters created with continuous provisioning, enabling you to proactively verify GPU accelerator health on running instances at any time. Continuous provisioning lets you start training quickly and scale instance groups asynchronously without all-or-nothing failures, and you can now pair that flexibility with comprehensive hardware validation as instances come online. This capability addresses a critical challenge

items.title: Amazon SageMaker Feature Store now supports batch feature writes and record listing
items.content_snippet: Amazon SageMaker Feature Store is a fully managed capability that makes it easy to compute, store, and retrieve features for training and deploying AI models. SageMaker Feature Store now supports new capabilities for high-throughput feature ingestion, record discovery, and offline store cataloging. Data scientists can now write multiple records across multiple feature groups in a single request with BatchWriteRecord, list the records stored in a feature group without knowing each record identifi

items.title: Amazon MSK Replicator now supports replication from external Apache Kafka clusters to MSK Standard brokers
items.content_snippet: Amazon MSK Replicator now supports data replication from external Apache Kafka clusters - including on-premises, self-managed on AWS, or other cloud providers to Amazon MSK Standard brokers. This capability extends replication support to MSK Standard brokers, in addition to the existing support for MSK Express brokers. With this launch, you can migrate workloads to MSK Standard brokers, support disaster recovery by using MSK clusters as a failover or backup target, and enable data distribution a

items.title: AWS Config now supports 191 additional managed rules
items.content_snippet: AWS Config now supports 191 additional managed rules across key services including Amazon Bedrock, Amazon SageMaker, Amazon ECS, Amazon EKS, Amazon RDS, Amazon Redshift, Amazon S3, and Amazon CloudTrail. This expansion increases built-in governance coverage across AI workloads and core cloud infrastructure. Examples of the new managed rules include evaluating resource configurations for encryption, logging, public access, network security, data protection, and other operational best practices ac

items.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
items.content_snippet: In this customer story, Jonathan Abrahamson, Chief Product & Digital Officer at Deutsche Telekom, shares how the company is reimagining telecommunications with AI—transforming employee workflows, customer service, network operations, and the future of voice. Hear how Deutsche Telekom is working toward becoming an AI-native telecommunications company, bringing AI into communications experiences through capabilities such as live translation, in-call assistants, and automated summarization, while r

items.title: ChatGPT Work | How NVIDIA Scales GTM with AI
items.content_snippet: “ChatGPT has really been a force multiplier” Will Daney, Go-To-Market Strategist at NVIDIA, uses ChatGPT Work to: 🤖 Automate repeatable workflows ⏱️ Eliminate manual analysis 📈 Turn customer data into actionable insights “You build the building block yourself, and then it can scale to your global team pretty easily.” Check out how ChatGPT Work can work for you: http://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/

items.title: ChatGPT Work | Zapier: Seven Figures in Monthly Pipeline
items.content_snippet: “We quantified the impact of this project to the tune of seven figures in pipeline every single month.” Angela Ferrante, Head of Enterprise Marketing at Zapier, used ChatGPT Work to automate a process that once took 35–45 minutes per lead. Now, her team can QA thousands of inbound leads, recover missed opportunities, and spend more time on strategic work. Check out how ChatGPT Work can work for you: http://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/

items.title: Get started with ChatGPT Work
items.content_snippet: ChatGPT Work gives you one place to delegate real work across your apps, files, local projects, browser, and computer. It works across web, mobile, and the new ChatGPT desktop app. In this walkthrough, see how to connect your tools, set up recurring work, move between devices, and collaborate with ChatGPT as it works toward an outcome. Get started: https://learn.chatgpt.com/ Chapters: 00:00 Introducing ChatGPT Work 00:45 Scheduled tasks 01:42 Marketing workflow 02:37 Data analytics workflow 03:4

items.title: ChatGPT Work for Marketing Teams

See how ChatGPT Work can help teams move from insight to brief to campaign-ready work https://openai.com/business/solutions/marketing/

items.title: The Golden Age of AI Engineering — Alexander Embiricos & Romain Huet & Peter Steinberger, OpenAI
items.content_snippet: OpenAI's Dev Day 2024 demo ran on an o1 preview model that could not run or check its own code, so Romain Huet had to cross his fingers live on stage. A year later, the same kind of demo ran a full camera and lighting rig, because the model could now test its own work. Alexander Embiricos and Huet use that jump to show how fast Codex is moving: new model releases went from every 15 months to about every 6 weeks. They walk through why Codex is built on the same responses API, open source harness,
